key_insight: Bregman ADMM can avoid strict-saddle KKT points almost surely in certain nonconvex, non-Lipschitz problems, giving second-order stationarity guarantees under two-sided relative smoothness.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more reliable optimization framework for large-scale matrix, tensor, and distributed consensus models where standard Lipschitz-gradient assumptions fail.
method: Models the problem with a Bregman kernel and two-sided relative smoothness instead of global gradient Lipschitzness.
Shows the Bregman ADMM update defines a smooth primal-dual fixed-point map on an invariant domain.
Uses dynamical-systems analysis to prove strict-saddle KKT points are unstable, and extends the argument to star-consensus multi-block distributed optimization.
evidence_signal: The paper supports the theory with numerical experiments on distributed matrix factorization and a symmetric tensor factorization example.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on an invariant open state-space domain and specific structural assumptions, so practical performance may depend on careful kernel and initialization choices.
paper_title: Second-Order KKT Guarantees for Bregman ADMM in Nonconvex and Non-Lipschitz Optimization
